articles.title: Periscope Equity Makes Strategic Investment in Amusement Connect Payments Platform
articles.summary: Private equity firm Periscope Equity has made a strategic investment in Amusement Connect, a specialty payments platform built specifically for the amusement industry. The deal signals growing institutional investor interest in FEC-focused fintech infrastructure. For operators, this investment could accelerate product development and broader adoption of purpose-built payment solutions across arcades and FECs.

articles.title: ROLLER Partners with Adyen to Offer Embedded Financing to 3,000+ Venues
articles.summary: Venue management platform ROLLER has partnered with global payments giant Adyen to provide fast, embedded financing through a Capital product available to more than 3,000 venues on its platform. The integration allows FEC operators to access working capital directly within the software they already use, reducing friction in funding expansions or equipment upgrades. This is a significant fintech development for small and mid-size FEC operators who often struggle with traditional lending.

articles.title: TicketSpice Named Ticketing Platform for All Eight Enchanted Parks Locations
articles.summary: TicketSpice has been selected to power admissions ticketing across all eight Enchanted Parks venues, the portfolio formed after a $330 million acquisition of former Six Flags properties. The deal highlights growing competition among ticketing platforms for large multi-venue FEC and theme park operators. For FEC operators evaluating ticketing solutions, this endorsement from a major new parks group provides a useful benchmark.

articles.title: Urban Air Adventure Parks Expands Into Maryland with New Indoor Entertainment Location
articles.summary: Urban Air continues its aggressive national franchise expansion with a new Maryland location, reinforcing the brand's position as one of the fastest-growing indoor FEC chains in the country. The opening adds to a growing footprint that competes directly with local trampoline parks, adventure centers, and family entertainment venues. Independent FEC operators in the region should take note of the competitive landscape shift.

articles.title: K1 Speed Opens Multi-Level Indoor Go-Kart Track in Thornton, Colorado
articles.summary: K1 Speed has opened a new indoor amusement center in Thornton, Colorado, featuring a multi-level go-kart track as its anchor attraction. The opening continues K1 Speed's national expansion of upscale indoor karting venues targeting adults and corporate events. The multi-level format is an increasingly popular design solution for operators looking to maximize entertainment density in limited footprints.

articles.title: Private Equity Firm Periscope Invests in Amusement Connect Payments Platform
articles.summary: Periscope Equity's investment in Amusement Connect underscores the attractiveness of niche B2B software serving the amusement and FEC sector to institutional investors. Amusement Connect provides specialty payment processing tools tailored to arcade and amusement operators. The backing from a PE firm suggests expectations of significant growth and platform expansion in the space.

articles.title: Six Flags Fiesta Texas Unveils Werewolf Gorge, World's Longest Family Launch Coaster
articles.summary: Six Flags Fiesta Texas has announced Werewolf Gorge, billed as the world's longest family launch roller coaster, featuring four launches, immersive storytelling, and a 39-inch height requirement designed to maximize multi-generational ridership. The attraction reflects a broader industry trend of investing in family-accessible thrill rides that appeal to a wider demographic than traditional coasters. FEC operators can draw inspiration from the emphasis on storytelling and accessible thrills.

articles.title: Activate Expands Nordic Footprint with First Denmark Location
articles.summary: Activate, the tech-infused competitive socializing concept, is opening its first Denmark location, continuing its rapid international expansion across Europe. The brand's format, which combines physical activity with interactive technology challenges, sits squarely in the active entertainment segment competing with escape rooms and laser tag venues. FEC operators should monitor Activate's growth as a signal of sustained consumer demand for tech-driven competitive experiences.

articles.title: How AI Is Transforming Operations and Guest Experience at Attractions
articles.summary: A detailed industry overview examines how theme parks and visitor attractions are deploying artificial intelligence across guest personalization, operational safety, and immersive storytelling. Highlights include Six Flags' AI concierge and drowning-prevention systems, Disney's robotic droids, and museum applications. FEC operators looking to modernize should view this as a practical roadmap for near-term AI adoption.

articles.title: Indoor Entertainment Center Signs Lease for Former Sears Space at Sunrise Mall
articles.summary: An indoor entertainment center has secured a lease for a portion of a former Sears anchor space at Sunrise Mall, reflecting the continued conversion of dead retail square footage into experiential entertainment venues. The trend of FECs backfilling defunct department store spaces is accelerating across the country as mall owners seek traffic-driving tenants. Operators and investors evaluating real estate opportunities should watch this segment closely.

articles.title: Madison's 'The Gallery' Entertainment District Set to Break Ground This Summer
articles.summary: A new entertainment district called The Gallery is set to break ground in Madison this summer, adding to the growing pipeline of mixed-use entertainment destination projects across mid-size U.S. markets. The project signals continued developer confidence in experiential entertainment as an anchor use for new commercial development. FEC operators and investors should monitor these entertainment district projects as potential partnership or leasing opportunities.

articles.title: Thunderzone Family Entertainment Center Opens in Carrollton, Georgia
articles.summary: A new family entertainment center called Thunderzone has opened in Carrollton, Georgia, adding another venue to the growing roster of independently operated FECs in smaller markets. The opening reflects a sustained entrepreneurial appetite for launching FEC concepts in communities underserved by larger chains. Industry suppliers and franchise brands may find this market segment an attractive growth opportunity.

articles.title: New Family Entertainment Center Planned for Mt. Juliet, Tennessee
articles.summary: A family entertainment center is slated to open in Mt. Juliet, Tennessee, continuing a pattern of new FEC development in fast-growing Nashville suburban communities. The project adds to a robust national pipeline of new venue openings in secondary and suburban markets. FEC suppliers and equipment vendors should note the ongoing demand for new builds in the Southeast.

articles.title: Chuck E. Cheese Partners with Asmodee for Spot It! Summer Gaming Experience
articles.summary: Chuck E. Cheese has teamed up with board game publisher Asmodee to bring a Spot It!-branded summer experience to its locations, blending tabletop gaming IP with the FEC environment. The partnership highlights an emerging strategy of cross-promotional licensing to drive incremental family visits during competitive summer months. FEC operators can take note of how branded IP partnerships can differentiate seasonal programming.

articles.title: Spokane Valley FEC Closes Permanently After Two Years in Business
articles.summary: A family entertainment center in Spokane Valley has permanently closed its doors just two years after opening, raising questions about the sustainability of new FEC ventures in competitive or undercapitalized markets. The closure follows a similar shutdown at another Spokane-area venue, suggesting localized market saturation or operational challenges. FEC operators and investors should use these closures as a reminder to rigorously evaluate market demand and unit economics before launch.

articles.title: Play CENLA Family Entertainment Center Shuts Down in Louisiana
articles.summary: Play CENLA, a family entertainment center in central Louisiana, has closed its doors, adding to a recent string of FEC closures across smaller U.S. markets. Operational and financial pressures continue to challenge independent FEC operators, particularly those without diversified revenue streams. The closure underscores the importance of strong programming, membership models, and community engagement for long-term viability.

articles.title: Slick City Indoor Slide Park Expanding to Milford This Summer
articles.summary: Slick City, the rapidly expanding indoor slide park concept, is opening a new location in Milford, continuing its growth into suburban markets across the country. The brand's format of large-format indoor slides appeals to the same family demographic served by trampoline parks and indoor adventure centers. Competing FEC operators should be aware of Slick City's expansion as a new form of active indoor entertainment vying for the same customer.

articles.title: IAAPA Expo Asia 2026 Preview: What Attractions Professionals Need to Know
articles.summary: IAAPA Expo Asia 2026 takes place June 9-12 at the Hong Kong Convention and Exhibition Centre, featuring over 20 EDUSessions on major industry trends and a broad exhibitor showcase. The event brings together attractions professionals from across the Asia-Pacific region and beyond to explore new products, technologies, and best practices. FEC operators and suppliers with international interests or looking for emerging product trends should consider attending.

articles.title: Martin & Vleminckx to Showcase Compact Thrill Rides at IAAPA Expo Asia 2026
articles.summary: Ride manufacturer Martin & Vleminckx will exhibit at IAAPA Expo Asia 2026 in Hong Kong, showcasing attractions including the space-efficient Polercoaster and the interactive Unicoaster 2.0. The Polercoaster's vertical format is particularly relevant for FEC operators with limited footprints looking to add high-impact thrill rides. The show provides a key procurement opportunity for operators sourcing new attractions.

articles.title: Dellshire Resort Opens in Wisconsin Dells with Immersive Entertainment Centerpiece
articles.summary: Dellshire Resort has opened in Wisconsin Dells, featuring a large fire-breathing dragon as a central entertainment and theming element at the new property. The opening adds to the dense entertainment ecosystem in Wisconsin Dells, one of the country's most competitive family leisure markets. FEC operators can draw inspiration from the resort's use of dramatic, landmark experiential features to drive destination appeal.

articles.title: H. Betti Industries Appoints Michael Arnone as Senior Marketing Director
articles.summary: H. Betti Industries, a major amusement equipment distributor, has named Michael Arnone as Senior Director of Marketing, bringing over 20 years of brand strategy and digital marketing experience to the role. Leadership changes at key industry suppliers can signal shifts in go-to-market strategy and product focus that affect FEC operators as buyers. Operators who work with H. Betti should anticipate potential changes in how the company communicates and supports its customer base.

articles.title: Kids Bowl Free Program Returns This Summer at Kentucky and Southern Indiana Lanes
articles.summary: Several bowling centers across Kentucky and Southern Indiana are participating in the Kids Bowl Free summer program, offering complimentary lanes to children as a traffic-driving promotional strategy. The program is a widely used summer activation tool that helps bowling operators fill off-peak lanes and build youth loyalty. FEC operators with bowling components should evaluate participation in structured summer programs to boost family visitation.

articles.title: Longtime Menasha, Wisconsin Bowling Alley Closes Permanently
articles.summary: Sabre Lanes in Menasha, Wisconsin has permanently closed after years in operation, with ownership directing bowlers to a sister facility in nearby Appleton. The closure reflects ongoing consolidation pressures in the traditional bowling sector as standalone lanes struggle to compete with full-service FEC formats. Bowling industry stakeholders should monitor the pace of single-purpose lane closures as a barometer of sector health.

articles.title: Southern California FEC Targets Birthday Party Search Demand with New Positioning
articles.summary: A Southern California family entertainment operator has issued a release addressing the high volume of local families searching for birthday party venues, signaling a strategic focus on capturing event-driven revenue. Birthday parties remain one of the highest-margin revenue streams for FECs, making this positioning relevant for operators nationwide. The release highlights how digital search behavior can inform FEC marketing and package strategy.

articles.title: OKANA Resort Fully Opens for First Summer Season in Oklahoma City
articles.summary: OKANA Resort in Oklahoma City has reached full operational status for its inaugural summer season, offering a major new mixed-use entertainment and lodging destination for the region. The resort represents a growing trend of destination entertainment resorts combining water parks, lodging, and diverse FEC-style attractions in a single complex. Operators and investors tracking large-format entertainment resort development should follow OKANA's performance as an early indicator of viability in the Midwest.

articles.title: Wild Waves Theme Park to Close After Nearly 50 Years of Operation
articles.summary: Wild Waves Theme Park in the Pacific Northwest is set to permanently close after nearly five decades, marking a significant loss for the regional amusement industry. The closure of a long-standing regional park serves as a cautionary signal about the financial sustainability challenges facing mid-size independent parks. FEC operators and investors should analyze the contributing factors as a case study in long-term venue viability.
